headline: Adobe Buyback And AI Partnerships Reframe Enterprise Growth Opportunity
summary: Adobe (NasdaqGS:ADBE) approved a new US$25b share repurchase authorization, running through 2030. The company announced new AI driven marketing partnerships with DICK'S Sporting Goods and Xfinity at its 2026 Summit. Adobe also expanded relationships with Omnicom and IBM to develop vertical specific AI agent solutions for enterprises. Adobe, best known for Creative Cloud and Experience Cloud, is leaning deeper into AI for marketing and content production as large enterprises look to automate...

headline: Trump Cease-Fire Extension Lifts Stocks. Why Markets Are Still on Edge.
summary: U.S. stocks are set to advance through another extended deadline on peace talks with Iran, with markets poised for solid gains following their first two-day decline in three weeks.  President Donald Trump effectively issued an indefinite suspension of his Wednesday deadline for talks with Iran after the close of trading on Tuesday, declaring that the U.S. will hold off on attacking the nation until a new proposal is put forward, or “discussions are concluded, one way or the other.”  “Based on the fact that the Government of Iran is seriously fractured, not unexpectedly so, we have been asked to hold our Attack on the Country of Iran until such time as their leaders and representatives can come up with a unified proposal,” Trump said.

content.title: Trump Cease-Fire Extension Lifts Stocks. Why Markets Are Still on Edge.

headline: IBM Earnings Are on Deck. Wall Street Expects Revenue Growth.
summary: International Business Machines  reports earnings Wednesday night following a difficult year for the stock amid broad concerns over how artificial intelligence will affect software companies.  Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect  IBM  to report adjusted earnings of $1.81 a share on revenue of $15.6 billion.  Wall street expects revenue in the company’s software segment to have grown 10% from the prior year to $6.98 billion.

content.title: IBM Earnings Are on Deck. Wall Street Expects Revenue Growth.
content.summary: International Business Machines  reports earnings Wednesday night following a difficult year for the stock amid broad concerns over how artificial intelligence will affect software companies.  Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect  IBM  to report adjusted earnings of $1.81 a share on revenue of $15.6 billion.  Wall street expects revenue in the company’s software segment to have grown 10% from the prior year to $6.98 billion.

headline: IBM stock drops despite beating Q1 earnings estimates
summary: IBM (IBM) is out with its first quarter results after Wednesday's closing bell, topping Wall Street's earnings ($1.91 per share vs. expectations of $1.81) and revenue estimates. ($15.92 billion vs. expectations of $15.67 billion). Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton highlights IBM's earnings release as the stock drops in after-hours trading.

content.title: IBM stock drops despite beating Q1 earnings estimates
content.description:
IBM (IBM) is out with its first quarter results after Wednesday's closing bell, topping Wall Street's earnings ($1.91 per share vs. expectations of $1.81) and revenue estimates. ($15.92 billion vs. expectations of $15.67 billion).

Market Domination Overtime Host Josh Lipton highlights IBM's earnings release as the stock drops in after-hours trading.

headline: IBM Stock Slides Late Despite Earnings Ahead Of Expectations
summary: IBM posted adjusted earnings and revenue growth ahead of expectations for its March quarter.  Meanwhile, IBM reiterated its previous forecast for 2026 full-year constant currency revenue growth of more than 5%.  "As clients scale use cases, AI continues to be a tailwind for our global business," Chief Executive Arvind Krishna said in a news release.

headline: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Beats Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations But Stock Drops
summary: Technology and consulting giant IBM (NYSE:IBM) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 9.5% year on year to $15.92 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.91 per share was 5.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: IBM (NYSE:IBM) Beats Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations But Stock Drops
content.summary: Technology and consulting giant IBM (NYSE:IBM) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 9.5% year on year to $15.92 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.91 per share was 5.5% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: IBM tops quarterly estimates on hybrid cloud growth
summary: IBM beat first-quarter profit estimates on Wednesday as artificial intelligence adoption boosted demand for its software services used ‌for managing large amounts of data and automating IT processes.  Enterprise demand for generative AI and hybrid ​cloud, which lets companies run applications and store data across their own data centers and public clouds, is surging as businesses automate workflows.  IBM's software segment, anchored by its high-margin hybrid cloud unit Red Hat, and a suite of ‌AI tools under the Watsonx ⁠brand, grew 11.3% in the first quarter.

content.title: IBM tops quarterly estimates on hybrid cloud growth
content.summary: IBM beat first-quarter profit estimates on Wednesday as artificial intelligence adoption boosted demand for its software services used ‌for managing large amounts of data and automating IT processes.  Enterprise demand for generative AI and hybrid ​cloud, which lets companies run applications and store data across their own data centers and public clouds, is surging as businesses automate workflows.  IBM's software segment, anchored by its high-margin hybrid cloud unit Red Hat, and a suite of ‌AI tools under the Watsonx ⁠brand, grew 11.3% in the first quarter.
